creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
^Triage: If there is a changelog or release notes URL available for this version, please add it to the URL field.

^Triage: Maintainer-feedback flag (+) not required unless requested (?) first.

Q/A:  Makefile: new ports should not set PORTREVISION.

Some variables are out of order:

# USES block                         
USES                                         
USE_RC_SUBR            
                                                            
-MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE                    
                                                            
# Configure block
GNU_CONFIGURE                                         
CONFIGURE_ARGS                          
                                                            
# Make block                            
+MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE                                                                                                        
-RADMINUSER                                                                                                              
                                                            
# Standard bsd.port.mk variables
+SUB_FILES                              
+SUB_LIST          
                                                            
# Users and groups block
USERS         
GROUPS                                    
                                                            
-RADMIND_BASE_DIR             
                                                            
-SUB_LIST                                      
                                                            
# Packaging list block                
PLIST_SUB                                                                                                                
                                                            
# Options definitions                            
+OPTIONS_DEFINE                          
-SUB_FILES                             

What are we using PORTREVISION for? Do upstream roll new versions without bumping the version number?

Thanks!
